highlights.text: I wrote on Monday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I wrote at the close yesterday: “My general is always to defer to the trend.
Their footprint is standardized and takes the form of my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Price sells rapidly in an “elevator down” flush and loses a low.
When you understand this, you realize your job is not to predict where price is going or how, but simply to respond when price puts in the Failed Breakdown.
Recovered, Failed Breakdown, ran to 6523 Monday evening.
We sold yesterday morning rapidly (elevator down), lost 6495 by a few points, Failed Breakdown, then squeezed to 6531 exact by the close.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss the Failed Breakdown on Friday that gave us the monster 75 point squeeze into yesterday’s highs (this is important to know), then I’ll talk the actionable trade plan for tomorrow.
6495 to 6518= a chop range with 6505 magnet.
6518 to 6494 remains the range with 6505 magnet.

post_title: SPX Makes Big New All Time Highs. Will CPI Tomorrow Ruin It? September 11 Plan
